voiceNote: Received Pronunciation with upper-class register — control is the primary instrument

emotionStates.label: Cold Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Clipped consonants, measured pace, minimal inflection — the quieter the voice, the more dangerous

emotionStates.label: Dark Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Slower pace, lower pitch, slight breathiness on vowels — control applied to want

emotionStates.label: Contempt
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Precise diction, rising inflection on declarative statements as if correcting a child, no acceleration

emotionStates.label: Rare Tenderness
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Measured pace softened at edges, consonants lose their clip — this voice is unfamiliar in their own mouth

emotionStates.label: Command
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Flat affect, no question inflection on any sentence regardless of phrasing — assumes compliance

emotionStates.label: Suppressed Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Overly controlled, too-precise diction, barely perceptible pause before proper nouns that carry weight

voiceNote: Connaught lilt, melodic and unhurried — emotion moves through the music of the sentence

emotionStates.label: Dark Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Lilt deepens, sentences trail into warmth — the music of the accent becomes the seduction itself

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Melodic pattern flattens, rhythm slows until each word lands separately

emotionStates.label: Fierce Loyalty
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Rising cadence, emphasis on relational words — 'mine,' 'ours,' 'yours' — with no softening

emotionStates.label: Dark Humor
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Quick pace, the punch delivered in a falling tone as if it's simply obvious

emotionStates.label: Warning
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Lilt goes flat and still — the music stops entirely, the words come out plain and absolute

emotionStates.label: Tenderness
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Pace drops to conversational minimum, the lilt at full warmth, soft on the final syllable of key words

voiceNote: Highland Scots, rolling R's and open vowels — a voice shaped by difficult terrain and difficult silence

emotionStates.label: Controlled Rage
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Rolling R's on threat words, jaw-set vowels, clipped endings — the burr becomes a growl

emotionStates.label: Reluctant Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Slower pace, open vowels held longer, the R's soften on words that matter most

emotionStates.label: Dry Wit
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Deadpan delivery, flat affect until the final word which carries the whole weight of the joke

emotionStates.label: Protectiveness
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Low, unhurried, steady — the voice of a person who does not need to raise it to be certain

emotionStates.label: Ancient Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Pace drops, the rolling R's become rarer, pauses between phrases where the words run dry

emotionStates.label: Battle Readiness
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Clipped, efficient, all softness stripped — every word a tool, nothing decorative

voiceNote: Standard Parisian French accent in English — precision weaponized as seduction

emotionStates.label: Seduction
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Gliding vowels, precise consonants that make every word feel chosen — the accent itself does most of the work

emotionStates.label: Intellectual Contempt
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Formal diction, sentences completed fully even when they wound — the courtesy is the cruelty

emotionStates.label: Boredom
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Rising intonation on declaratives, as if nothing merits full attention — until it does

emotionStates.label: Possessive Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Precision increases, not decreases — anger makes the diction more correct, not less

emotionStates.label: Unexpected Vulnerability
emotionStates.prosodyNote: English idioms used imperfectly in moments of genuine feeling — the language slips when the armor does

emotionStates.label: Pleasure
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Pace slows luxuriously, each sense-word given space — nothing rushed, everything tasted

voiceNote: Ambiguous Slavic origin — the accent implies centuries and distance from anything mortal

emotionStates.label: Ancient Authority
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Formal grammar, archaic word choices, deliberate pace — someone for whom English is merely the current language, not the native one

emotionStates.label: Dark Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Slightly sibilant on desire-words, vowels opened and held — the accent becomes otherworldly in hunger

emotionStates.label: Cold Calculation
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Precise enunciation, no contractions, each sentence structured as if for a legal document

emotionStates.label: Rare Warmth
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Formality drops partially — contractions appear, word order softens, as if the person beneath the centuries is briefly visible

emotionStates.label: Threat
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Volume drops, pace drops, the accent thickens as if ancestral memory is asserting itself

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Language becomes more formal under grief, not less — retreating into older syntax as armor

voiceNote: Mississippi or Alabama — sweetness as camouflage for edges that cut deeper for being unexpected

emotionStates.label: Dangerous Charm
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Slow drawl, warmth on the surface, emphasis on courtesy words that function as warning — 'bless your heart' with intent

emotionStates.label: Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Drawl accelerates, vowels sharpen, the sweetness stripped away to reveal what it was covering

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The drawl slows further, words given more space, the warmth becomes something older — a lament register

emotionStates.label: Stubborn Refusal
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Voice takes on a polite firmness that will not shift — sweetness maintained while communicating absolute immovability

emotionStates.label: Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Drawl deepens, pace drops to a crawl on certain words — the politeness is exactly what makes it devastating

emotionStates.label: Threat
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The most dangerous register: sweet, unhurried, very specific about consequences, never raised above a conversational tone

voiceNote: Mountain Scots-Irish inheritance — direct, imagistic, with a relationship to old speech patterns

emotionStates.label: Earned Trust
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Fewer words than needed, each one chosen — the accent at its most plainspoken and thereby most sincere

emotionStates.label: Warning
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Rhythm becomes biblical, old patterns surfacing — cadence of someone who has seen enough to know

emotionStates.label: Rage
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Short sentences, hard stops, no figurative language — stripped to the essential

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Imagistic language, indirect approach — grief named by its surroundings rather than its feeling

emotionStates.label: Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The directness becomes a different kind of honesty — plain speech about wanting is more intimate than ornamentation

emotionStates.label: Stubbornness
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Silence used as complete sentences, followed by one declarative that ends all discussion

voiceNote: Working-class New York — speed as defense, wit as weapon, vulnerability the thing most carefully concealed

emotionStates.label: Defensive Wit
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Fast pace, punchy rhythm, the joke landing before the listener has finished parsing the setup

emotionStates.label: Genuine Anger
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Pace accelerates, volume drops — the most serious anger speaks quietly and quickly

emotionStates.label: Unexpected Sincerity
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Pace slows abruptly, pace unlike the rest of the character's speech — sincerity announced by its own awkwardness

emotionStates.label: Dark Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The wit goes quiet, the pace slows — the accent stripped of performance, something raw underneath

emotionStates.label: Protectiveness
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Short, declarative, no qualifiers — the city instinct to handle things directly, applied to care

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The words come out wrong at first — the speed that usually serves as armor is unavailable, and what's left is unguarded

voiceNote: Swedish or Norwegian cadence — melodic sentence music, precision, and a relationship with silence as meaning

emotionStates.label: Clinical Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Precise word choices for feeling, the sentence music making clinical words sensual against their intent

emotionStates.label: Ancient Calm
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Even cadence, no acceleration, silence between statements as deliberate punctuation

emotionStates.label: Rare Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Cadence breaks — sentences shorten, the music goes flat, the precision increases dangerously

emotionStates.label: Loneliness
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Sentences trail at their ends, the characteristic rise-fall of the cadence flattens, replies shorter than usual

emotionStates.label: Dark Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The sentence music slows, vowels open — the precision of word choice makes the desire more acute, not less

emotionStates.label: Dark Humor
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Deadpan, delivered with full cadence as if it were an ordinary observation — the humor arrives in retrospect

voiceNote: Moscow educated Russian — formal grammar, the consonants carrying weight, emotions compressed into precise vocabulary

emotionStates.label: Cold Authority
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Hard consonants, formal grammar — commands phrased as statements, no softening

emotionStates.label: Suppressed Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The formal register maintained while word choices become more specific, more sensory — control's effort is visible

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Russian has words for grief with no English equivalent; the character reaches for them and fails, leaving gaps

emotionStates.label: Dark Humor
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Delivered as pure fact, without inflection — the darkness is the joke, and the straightfaced delivery is the punchline

emotionStates.label: Loyalty
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Few words, absolutely certain — the restraint of the language is the measure of the feeling

emotionStates.label: Threat
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Extremely quiet, very formal, no contractions, no idiom — the threat is the precision itself

voiceNote: Barbadian or Jamaican educated — rhythm as emotional register, language that moves like water or cuts like coral

emotionStates.label: Joyful Menace
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The rhythm carries both warmth and edge simultaneously — the music of the accent is exactly the point

emotionStates.label: Wisdom
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Slower cadence, imagistic — the sentence finds its destination by a different path than expected

emotionStates.label: Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Rhythm tightens, creole constructions surface even in careful speakers — the native pattern emerging under pressure

emotionStates.label: Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The rhythm deepens and slows — the language at full musicality is the most intimate register

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Rhythm becomes sparse, the characteristic music gone — grief is when the pattern breaks

emotionStates.label: Warning
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Cadence becomes measured and absolutely clear — no idiom, no warmth, the information delivered like a gift the recipient will regret

voiceNote: Australian English — laconic, direct, understatement as primary emotional mode

emotionStates.label: Deflective Humor
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The understatement is the joke — massive things named with tiny words, serious tone maintained throughout

emotionStates.label: Genuine Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The understatement drops, sentences lengthen into direct statements — when an Australian stops being laconic, the danger is real

emotionStates.label: Loyalty
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Not named or performed — expressed through action described in the same flat register as everything else

emotionStates.label: Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The understatement becomes almost painful — enormous feeling described in one plain sentence

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The laconic register maintained even here — which makes it more devastating, not less

emotionStates.label: Threat
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Friendly tone, specific detail, complete certainty — the colloquial register is exactly what makes it land

voiceNote: Indian or Pakistani educated English — formal precision, layered meaning, a relationship with respectful indirection

emotionStates.label: Formal Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Diction becomes more formal under anger, not less — the courtesy is weaponized, each word placed with care

emotionStates.label: Intellectual Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Ideas and wanting become one register — the formal precision applied to feeling makes it exact and devastating

emotionStates.label: Familial Weight
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Certain words carry whole histories — names, titles, obligations, spoken with awareness of their weight

emotionStates.label: Controlled Warmth
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Warmth within formal register — expressed through attentiveness and precision, never through sentiment

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Formal language continues but grows slower, more precise — grief as something that must be named correctly

emotionStates.label: Threat
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The most formal register, the most careful grammar — the threat expressed as a statement of fact with no emotional inflection

voiceNote: Standard German accent in English — compound thinking, precision as respect, directness not unkindness

emotionStates.label: Precision Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Emotion named directly, without metaphor — what the German character says they want, they mean exactly

emotionStates.label: Analytical Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Anger expressed as detailed enumeration of specific failures — methodical, complete, devastating

emotionStates.label: Unexpected Tenderness
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The direct register applied to care — precise, sincere, no ornamentation, which makes it feel more true than poetry

emotionStates.label: Dark Humor
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Delivered as a logical observation, the absurdity acknowledged without inflection

emotionStates.label: Loyalty
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Stated as a fact, binding as a contract — not performed, not questioned, not qualified

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: One word, sometimes, used precisely, then silence — or the clinical description of feeling as if naming it accurately is the only respect grief deserves

voiceNote: Castilian Spanish accent — formality with passion underneath, honor as a living obligation

emotionStates.label: Formal Passion
emotionStates.prosodyNote: High emotion expressed within formal register — the passion is present because the speaker chooses to name it, not because they perform it

emotionStates.label: Wounded Pride
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Formal diction maintained, volume drops — pride requires that the hurt not be visible, which makes it more visible

emotionStates.label: Dark Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The formality becomes ceremony — desire named with full gravity, as something the speaker takes responsibility for

emotionStates.label: Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Pace increases, the Castilian lisp on z/c sounds more pronounced — not performed, simply present under pressure

emotionStates.label: Honor Stated
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Deliberate, unhurried, the word 'honor' used as if it has weight — because in this register, it does

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Formal, slow, complete sentences — grief requires courtesy, requires completion, requires the person to be named fully

voiceNote: Northern or Roman Italian — gesture is implied in the speech rhythm, emotion expressed rather than concealed

emotionStates.label: Theatrical Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Pace and volume, sentences structured for maximum emotional delivery — but the intensity is sincere, not performed

emotionStates.label: Genuine Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Pace slows, vowels open — the Italian musicality fully engaged, nothing held back or strategic

emotionStates.label: Family Obligation
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The warmth of the register drops to something grave and heavy — family is not warm in this mode, it is binding

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Expansive, named, given full space — grief is not minimized or hidden but given appropriate scale

emotionStates.label: Dark Charm
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The musicality applied to intent that is not innocent — the charm and the danger arriving simultaneously

emotionStates.label: Warning
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The warmth of the accent maintained while the words become completely clear — warmth and warning, without contradiction

voiceNote: Welsh English — lyrical cadence, music native to the speech pattern, emotion that rises in the rhythm

emotionStates.label: Bardic Intensity
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The natural musicality of Welsh English cadence at full expression — story told as song

emotionStates.label: Dark Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The lyrical cadence slowed, the music underlying the words rather than riding over them

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Welsh has a native relationship with sung grief; the rhythm of lament is in the cadence even in English

emotionStates.label: Communal Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Anger that is not individual — the cadence carries the weight of 'we' even when 'I' is the word used

emotionStates.label: Dry Wit
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The lyrical register goes flat and then the joke arrives — the flatness is the windup

emotionStates.label: Pride
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The musicality rises — pride expressed through the cadence's full register, carrying something larger than the individual

voiceNote: Mandarin first-language speaker — tonal instincts in English phrasing, formal grammar, emotion expressed through precision

emotionStates.label: Controlled Formality
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Very precise grammar, minimal contractions — the formality is respect, and its withdrawal is a signal

emotionStates.label: Suppressed Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Word choices become more specific, more literary — the suppression visible in the care of language

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Chinese literary grief tends toward indirection — feeling named by its surroundings, by weather, by time, not by the feeling word itself

emotionStates.label: Rare Directness
emotionStates.prosodyNote: When formality drops entirely and a single plain sentence emerges — the rarity of the register signals its importance

emotionStates.label: Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The grammar becomes more formal, not less — fury addressed with extreme precision is more devastating than raised volume

emotionStates.label: Dark Humor
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Delivered as an observation about the situation's absurdity — the formality is the joke's best delivery mechanism

voiceNote: Japanese first-language English — politeness as grammar, emotional content carried in what is not said

emotionStates.label: Polite Intensity
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Extreme politeness carrying extreme weight — the register of extreme care applied to something that matters enormously

emotionStates.label: Suppressed Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: What is not said is louder than what is — the spaces in speech are as meaningful as the words

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Silence, then one precise word, then silence — Japanese grief is in the interval, not the expression

emotionStates.label: Rare Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Anger that breaks the politeness register is frightening precisely because the register has held so long

emotionStates.label: Indirect Warning
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Warning delivered as an observation, a gentle concern — its indirection is the register of someone who will not say the threat aloud but is very clear about it

emotionStates.label: Unexpected Directness
emotionStates.prosodyNote: One sentence, completely plain, completely sincere — the abandonment of indirection as the most intimate possible act

voiceNote: Egyptian or Lebanese educated English — poetry native to the language, hospitality as power, emotion that honors its own weight

emotionStates.label: Formal Hospitality
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Warmth as a formal offering — hospitality with weight and consequence, not casual welcome

emotionStates.label: Poetic Desire
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Arabic has a literary tradition of desire-speech — the imagery is not decoration but the primary vehicle

emotionStates.label: Grief
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Arabic grief has formal registers — the vocabulary of loss is specific and honored, not avoided

emotionStates.label: Fury
emotionStates.prosodyNote: Volume and cadence, the formal register maintained but pressed at its edges — fury as something that must be expressed completely

emotionStates.label: Dark Authority
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The hospitality register removed — what remains is older, stricter, with genealogical weight

emotionStates.label: Rare Vulnerability
emotionStates.prosodyNote: The formality drops and something careful and private is offered — the register of a person choosing to be seen
